scenario: A Cascadia Health operator runs Camunda 8.8 Operate and tries to cancel all running tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body that currently carries 1300 tokens. The operator needs to plan how many tokens will be affected by a single cancel or move action in this version.
question: How many tokens at most can be affected by a single cancel or move operation on this multi-instance scope in this version?

options.text: A single cancel or move operation affects at most 500 tokens within the flow node instance nested in the multi-instance body, so the operation must be repeated until all child tokens have been handled
A single cancel or move operation affects every running child token in the multi-instance body in one step;  the documented limit applies only to plain non multi-instance flow nodes in the instance
A single cancel or move operation affects at most 50 tokens at a time, so the operator must trigger 26 separate operations against the same flow node instance to cover all 1300 running tokens here
A single cancel or move operation affects at most 5000 tokens, so the entire 1300 token multi-instance body can always be processed in one cancel or one move action without any further repetition

a.text: Correct. The docs state that when cancelling or moving all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body, a maximum of 500 tokens can be affected; the modification can be repeated on the same flow node instance until all tokens have been handled.

b.text: Incorrect. The documented limit specifically applies when cancelling or moving all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body, so a single operation does not always cover every running child token in one step.

c.text: Incorrect. The documented per-operation cap on a multi-instance scope is 500 tokens, not 50, so the number of repeated operations needed to cover 1300 tokens is not 26.

d.text: Incorrect. The documented per-operation cap is 500 tokens for this case, not 5000; the 1300 token multi-instance body cannot be processed in a single cancel or move action under this limit.

explanation: For a cancel or move that targets all tokens within a single flow node instance nested in a multi-instance body, a maximum of 500 tokens can be affected per operation. The modification must be repeated on the same flow node instance until every token has been handled.
